Title: BN aims to wrest Johor Jaya from DAP after 13 years
Article:
PASIR GUDANG: Barisan Nasional (BN) is aiming to recapture Johor Jaya from DAP in the coming state election, an urban seat it last won 13 years ago.
Johor BN Youth chief Noor Azleen Ambros said the coalition was also determined to retain Permas.
Noor Azleen, who is also Pasir Gudang Umno Youth chief, said these were among the key issues discussed at the Pasir Gudang BN parliamentary meeting last night.
He said the meeting, chaired by Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin, focused on strategies to strengthen BN's machinery ahead of the polls.
Pasir Gudang MCA chairman Tan Tuan Peng and MIC chairman R. Vijaya were also present.
"Of course, our focus is to defend our victory in Permas and, at the same time, work towards recapturing Johor Jaya," Noor Azleen said.
"Both seats are crucial to contributing to the formation of a stable state government."
He said it did not matter who was nominated for the two seats, as the priority was to ensure BN's victory.
In the last state election, BN's Baharudin Mohamed Taib won the Permas seat with a majority of 7,956 votes.
In Johor Jaya, DAP's Liow Cai Tung retained the seat for a third term with a majority of 1,922 votes.
BN last won Johor Jaya in the 2008 election when MCA's Tan Cher Puk defeated a Parti Rakyat Malaysia candidate with a majority of 10,488 votes.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Motorcyclist killed in Jalan Reko crash
Article:
KAJANG: A 28-year-old motorcyclist was killed after he reportedly lost control of his motorcycle and fell into the opposite lane before being struck by an oncoming vehicle on Jalan Reko here.
District police chief Assistant Commissioner Naazron Abdul Yusof said the incident occurred about 11.13pm last night.
"The motorcyclist, a local technician, was travelling from Putrajaya towards his home in Semenyih when the incident happened.
"A 52-year-old e-hailing driver, travelling in the opposition direction, was unable to avoid him," he said in a statement.
Naazron said the motorcyclist was confirmed dead at the scene due to the severity of the impact.
He said the case is being investigated under Section 41(1) of the Road Transport Act for reckless or dangerous driving causing death, which carries a penalty of between five and 10 years in jail, a fine of up to RM50,000, and disqualification from holding a driving licence for at least five years upon conviction.
Those with information can contact the traffic investigation officer, Inspector Mohd Raziman Rasid, at 019-4565502.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Zahid proposes dedicated accreditation body for TVET institutions
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has proposed the establishment of a dedicated accreditation body for Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) institutions, separate from existing academic and skills accreditation agencies.
Zahid, who is also National TVET Council chairman, said the move was necessary given the presence of 1,395 TVET institutions nationwide.
"The best approach is for accreditation to be carried out by a dedicated body without interfering with the accreditation functions currently undertaken by the Malaysian Qualifications Agency (MQA) and the Skills Development Department (JPK)," he said after officiating the High-Impact Industry Forum here today.
He said several developed countries already operated separate accreditation bodies for technical education, a model Malaysia could emulate.
On the timeline for its establishment, Zahid said he had discussed the matter with Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abd Kadir.
"Zambry has given his commitment and the ministry's secretary-general will follow up with MQA and JPK," he said.
Zahid added that the new body would utilise existing personnel, with responsibilities focused specifically on technical education accreditation rather than requiring an entirely new workforce.
In 2024, he announced that the government had agreed to establish a single
accreditation body
for TVET programmes under the 12th Malaysia Plan.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Senior cop misconduct cases on IPCC radar, warns deputy IGP
Article:
KUCHING: Several integrity-related cases involving senior police officers ranked Senior Assistant Commissioner and above are now on the radar of the Independent Police Conduct Commission (IPCC).
Deputy Inspector-General of Police Tan Sri Ayob Khan Mydin Pitchay said he had received public complaints and that such cases be lodged with the IPCC.
He said police officers and personnel must no longer assume they are only being monitored by the Integrity and Standard Compliance Department, as the Home Ministry also has a dedicated agency overseeing them.
"There are currently two or three cases involving senior officers and I myself will be monitoring the progress of these cases handled by the IPCC.
"I will follow up to ensure that action is taken," he said when speaking at the launch of the book Strengthening Integrity, Empowering The Nation: Integrity As The Foundation Of Leadership here yesterday.
The book, written by Prof Datuk Dr Leonard Sabu, was jointly launched by Ayob and Sarawak Women, Children and Community Wellbeing Minister Datuk Seri Fatimah Abdullah.
Also present was former Sarawak Police Commissioner and special adviser to the book's publication, Datuk Mohd Yusoff Jaafar.
Ayob said the establishment of the IPCC shows that misconduct within the police force, particularly corruption-related issues, is no longer dealt with solely through internal channels.
"We should not think that having only the Integrity and Standard Compliance Department as an internal body makes us safe. There are now external agencies that also monitor and ensure action is taken against any wrongdoing," he said.
He said that integrity issues must be handled carefully and firmly to ensure continued public confidence in the police force.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Rafizi: Bersama to contest Johor and Negri Sembilan state elections
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Parti Bersama Malaysia leader Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli has confirmed that the party will contest in the upcoming Johor and Negri Sembilan state elections.
Speaking to reporters after setting up a public complaints and party membership registration counter at the Pandan Indah market here this morning, Rafizi said the party will begin accepting applications from prospective candidates next Monday (June 8) as part of its selection process, Sin Chew Daily reported.
He said Bersama has completed negotiations with local leaders in Johor, while discussions in Negri Sembilan are scheduled to begin next week.
"Essentially, we are matching our initial list of target seats with their views and assessments of the local situation," he said.
Rafizi added that the party will announce the constituencies it plans to contest in Johor and Negri Sembilan closer to nomination day.
When asked whether the three-week-old party would have sufficient time to prepare candidates for the elections, Rafizi said applications would open on Monday and all applicants would undergo interviews and assessments.
He said the process is aimed at ensuring that candidates selected by the party are not only qualified on paper but also capable of performing effectively on the ground.
The assessment process, he added, will also help prospective candidates better understand the challenges they may face if chosen to contest.
Rafizi said successful candidates will be required to sign a series of pledge documents and penalty agreements.
Describing himself as Bersama's spokesman, Rafizi said the party would formally announce its leadership structure only after winning seats in the elections.
"We do not want to be like other parties that spend their early days arguing over who the founder is. We are bypassing all that.
"Everything is moving simultaneously, and we will establish an organisational structure in due course," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Smuggling bid sinks in RM57.15mil drug bust in Muar
Article:
MUAR: Police have busted a syndicate believed to be smuggling drugs to a neighbouring country by sea, seizing narcotics worth RM57.15 million in a raid at Parit Amat Laut yesterday.
Region Two Marine Police commander Assistant Commissioner Noor Azman Jamal said the operation was launched following intelligence on smuggling activities in the area.
He said the raiding team spotted a boat and several vehicles at a coastal area off Muar shortly after midnight.
Several people were seen loading packages onto a boat believed to be bound for a neighbouring country.
"The raiding team moved in and detained a 63-year-old man and a 43-year-old woman, believed to be couriers.
"A thorough search of the boat and the vehicles led to the discovery of gunny sacks and black plastic bags containing drugs."
Noor Azman said the seizure comprised 549kg of syabu packed in 517 plastic bags, 191,268 ecstasy pills and 4,000 Yakuza liquid cartridges, with a total estimated value of RM57.15 million.
Police also seized seven vehicles worth RM204,487, a fibreglass sampan and two 85hp Yamaha Enduro outboard motors worth RM70,000, bringing the total value of the seizure to more than RM57.42 million.
The case is being investigated under Sections 39B and 15(1)(a) of the Dangerous Drugs Act, as well as Section 130V(1) of the Penal Code, for drug trafficking, self-administration and organised crime.
The offences carry lengthy prison terms, with the maximum punishment for drug trafficking being the death penalty or life imprisonment with whipping.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 1,576 sick, abandoned housing projects worth RM148bil revived since 2023
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The government has revived more than 1,576 sick and abandoned housing projects nationwide since 2023, with an estimated gross development value (GDV) of RM148.21 billion.
Housing and Local Government Minister Nga Kor Ming said the recovered projects involved 188,525 housing units.
Nga said this reflected the government's commitment to addressing longstanding issues involving stalled housing developments and protecting homebuyers affected by project delays.
He said this was also made possible through intervention by the ministry's Task Force on Sick and Abandoned Housing Projects.
He added that the Ministry aims to achieve zero abandoned housing projects by 2030.
"We will continue implementing the Madani Housing Reform agenda to address sick and abandoned housing projects more comprehensively," he said during the groundbreaking ceremony of the KL360 development.
Among the measures being introduced are mandatory audits of Housing Development Accounts (HDA), the strengthening of the Housing Integrated Management System (HIMS) and greater public use of the Teduh portal to verify developers and housing projects before making purchases.
"Our goal is clear: to ensure that every sen paid by homebuyers is used only for the development promised and that every house purchased is completed and delivered according to schedule," he said.
Separately, KL360, formerly known as M101 Skywheel, is a project that had stalled after facing financial difficulties and delays exacerbated by the pandemic.
Nga said the revival of the former M101 Skywheel project marked another significant success in the government's efforts to restore abandoned developments.
Originally launched as a RM1.5 billion landmark development comprising 1,511 units within a 79-storey tower, the project was planned to feature several iconic attractions, including a Sky Mall and a Ferris wheel at the top of the building.
However, the project was eventually abandoned due to multiple challenges, including the impact of the pandemic, prolonged construction delays and post-pandemic financial constraints.
"As a result, 337 purchasers were affected, involving sale and purchase agreements valued at more than RM306 million," he said.
The project was subsequently rescued by GD Properties, which stepped in as the white knight developer and worked with stakeholders to formulate a rehabilitation scheme.
The scheme was approved by the majority of buyers before receiving High Court endorsement in 2024.
KL360, a transit-oriented development located within walking distance of the MRT Raja Uda station, would comprise 785 serviced apartments, 221 office suites, commercial space and 40 lifestyle facilities featuring 16 different themes.
Nga said the integrated development, which will also feature a Sky Deck and Sky Glass Slide attraction, has the potential to become a vibrant and sustainable urban community while strengthening Kuala Lumpur's position as a competitive regional metropolis.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PM calls for specialised programmes to address talent pool readiness
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: Major investors, including Intel, Infineon and Amazon Web Services (AWS), have raised concerns about the readiness of Malaysia's talent pool, says Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.
He said the companies, which also include Nvidia, found that local trainees lacked exposure to complementary skill sets needed by the industry.
Anwar said this underscored the importance of stronger collaboration between the government and the private sector.
"I am not saying it cannot be done. Some of our educational institutions with hundreds of millions in funding can establish specialised programmes to address these needs," he said at the Hari TVET Negara celebrations today.
Anwar called for greater private sector participation in training programmes.
"There are companies, for example, that are major taxpayers generating significant profits, yet they contribute very little towards training our young people."
To address these gaps, Anwar announced a RM50 million allocation from the High Impact Programme Fund under the Skills Development Fund Corporation (PTPK) for the TVET 2.0 initiative.
The prime minister urged employers and industry players to work closely with the government, particularly the National TVET Council, to better coordinate training efforts.
He said it was important to ensure young workers were well-prepared for high-demand fields such as artificial intelligence (AI), cryptocurrency and renewable energy.
Also present was Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi, who is also chairman of the National TVET Council.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Pahang ruler briefed on federal allocations for the state, says Amir Hamzah
Article:
PETALING JAYA: Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan has briefed the Pahang ruler, Al-Sultan Abdullah Ri'ayatuddin Al-Mustafa Billah Shah, on Putrajaya's allocations to the state.
Amir Hamzah said that during an audience with Al-Sultan Abdullah last week, he explained that federal government allocations and development projects for Pahang had increased year-on-year.
"His Royal Highness also expressed his views on several other initiatives, which we will look into," he told reporters on the sidelines of the Micro Credit Turun Padang Programme.
"We will work to improve existing processes where possible," he said, after the event which was also attended by Treasury Secretary-General Tan Sri Johan Mahmood Merican.
Amir Hamzah said the government remained committed to developing Pahang through various initiatives and direct assistance for the people.
These include Sumbangan Tunai Rahmah and Sumbangan Asas Rahmah, as well as the East Coast Rail Link (ECRL), which is expected to be completed by the end of the year.
He said the ECRL was expected to catalyse new investments along the East Coast while boosting infrastructure development and industrial zones in the region.
"When the ECRL is completed, we will also focus on developing existing industrial zones on the East Coast.
"The infrastructure has been built, and we want to ensure more new industries can be brought into the area. We are also looking at how supporting infrastructure can be upgraded to facilitate investors using the provided facilities," he said.
On May 8, Tengku Mahkota Pahang Tengku Hassanal Ibrahim Alam Shah questioned the federal government's allocation to the state.
Following the remarks, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim instructed Amir Hamzah to brief Al-Sultan Abdullah on federal allocations for Pahang.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Mara TVET institutions lead with 99.5pct employability rate
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: Majlis Amanah Rakyat's (Mara) network of technical and vocational education and training (TVET) institutions lead the way with an employability rate of 99.5 per cent, said Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi.
Zahid congratulated Mara on the performance of all 270 of its TVET institutions, which he said were at the forefront of the national TVET network.
"They have achieved an employability rate of approximately 99.5 per cent for Mara TVET graduates," he said at the launch of TVET 2.0 at National TVET Day 2026.
Also in attendance was Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.
Zahid said that the employability rate of TVET graduates in general has climbed from 95.1 per cent to 98.7 per cent as of the end of May.
He added that the improvement is an achievement to be grateful for, but cautioned that sustaining and building on success is harder than achieving it.
"Our duty and responsibility is to defend that success and continue to improve upon it," he said.
Zahid also highlighted seven universities within the Malaysian Technical University Network (MTUN) that offered higher education pathways for TVET students all the way up to master's degree and PhD level, ensuring that the TVET route was not a ceiling but a launching pad for further academic achievement.
He added that the government had allocated RM7.9 billion to the TVET sector through the 2026 Budget, which is the highest allocation ever given, to strengthen the implementation of TVET 2.0, including upskilling and retraining programmes.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Myanmar teen caught with fake UNHCR card in Immigration raid
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: An alleged RM200 fake refugee card proved futile for a teenager who was detained during an Immigration operation at a squatter settlement along Jalan Klang Lama here early this morning.
The 19-year-old Myanmar teen was among those screened in Op Sapu where officers later discovered a suspected counterfeit United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR) card in his possession during the checks.
When questioned, the teenager said he had paid a friend to obtain the card about a year ago.
"The card was given to me by a friend, I had given him RM200 for it. I have been staying in the country since two years ago.
"I had entered Malaysia illegally via rat lanes. I previously worked at a shop selling vegetables in Ipoh before coming here to look for work," he said.
Immigration checks found the document is likely fake, and was used to support his stay and movement within the country.
Earlier, Immigration detained 78 undocumented migrants in a raid on a
squatter settlement
under Op Sapu.
They comprised 48 men and 30 women, including Indonesians, Myanmar nationals and an Indian national.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Mydin: Essential goods supply remains stable despite global uncertainties
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The supply of essential goods in Malaysia remains stable despite ongoing uncertainties in global supply chains, says Mydin Mohamed Holdings Bhd (Mydin) managing director Datuk Dr Ameer Ali Mydin.
He said careful planning by the retail sector and the smooth operation of the domestic agricultural industry have strengthened confidence that food supplies for the local market remain secure.
Ameer Ali said uninterrupted planting and harvesting, supported by sufficient agricultural inputs such as fertilisers, have helped maintain market stability, with no signs of shortages in the near future.
"At the moment, there are no indications of that. Planting and harvesting activities are proceeding as usual, while fertiliser supplies remain sufficient.
"We have also heard Agriculture and Food Security Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Sabu say that urea fertiliser supplies are adequate for the current planting season," he said on Bernama TV's Ruang Bicara programme titled 'Strengthening the Retail Industry, Driving the National Economy', here last night.
On imported goods, Ameer Ali said local retailers have adopted forward-buying strategies to ensure a consistent and adequate supply of essential imported products.
He also urged consumers not to resort to panic buying, noting that supply stability extends beyond food items to include products such as textiles and clothing.
"Supplies remain stable and adequate. There is no need to worry excessively, but at the same time, people should avoid wastage and unnecessary panic," he said.
Meanwhile, Ameer Ali welcomed the sizeable allocation for the Rahmah Cash Aid (STR) and Rahmah Basic Aid (SARA) programmes under Budget 2026, describing the initiative as having a multiplier effect on the domestic economy.
"Traders are also grateful because consumers' spending power is currently rather weak. When assistance such as this is provided, shops become busier and it creates a significant positive impact on the economy, especially for small businesses," he said.
In a separate development, Mydin is adapting to changing consumer lifestyles by opening more than 20 new outlets this year under a smaller community-store concept, offering a complete range of essential goods.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Police hunt Indonesian suspects over robbery, housebreaking cases in Manjung
Article:
IPOH:  Police are looking for a group of Indonesians believed to be behind eight robbery and housebreaking cases around Ayer Tawar and Pekan Gurney in Manjung this year.
Manjung police chief Assistant Commissioner Hasbullah Abd Rahman said the group, comprising three to four men, targeted homes located on the fringes of forests and in remote areas.
"The public are advised to ensure their homes are securely locked, enhance security features such as installing closed-circuit television (CCTV) cameras, and avoid keeping large amounts of cash at home," he said in a statement today.
He said those with information on this group should contact the Manjung district police headquarters operations room at 05-688 6222, the WhatsApp hotline at 017-682 8005, or any nearby police station to assist in investigations.
On June 1, Hasbullah said the group broke into the home of a woman in her 40s in Pekan Gurney where they threatened her with a machete before fleeing with cash and jewellery estimated to be worth RM10,000.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Talk things out, don't use courts,' says Zahid on Negri Sembilan crisis
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has called for the political crisis in Negri Sembilan involving the four Undang and its ruler Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir, to be resolved through negotiation rather than be brought before a tribunal or court.
"The way forward is to respect the sovereignty of the Dewan Keadilan dan Undang (DKU) that exists. In my view, the best path is through negotiation.
"There is no need to bring the matter before a tribunal or court," he told reporters after the opening ceremony for National TVET Day 2026 here today.
Yesterday, a video showing Tunku Nadzaruddin Tuanku Ja'afar being proclaimed as the
12th Yang di-Pertuan Besar of Negri Sembilan
surfaced on social media amid an ongoing constitutional dispute.
In the video, the proclamation was made by Jelebu Undang Datuk Maarof Mat Rashad on behalf of the Undang Yang Empat.
The video surfaced on the same day the Seremban High Court granted ad interim injunctions halting a planned special sitting of the Dewan Keadilan dan Undang Negeri Sembilan (DKU) and blocking any move to remove DKU secretary Raja Norazli Raja Nordin.
The injunctions were obtained by Raja Norazli and the DKU to preserve the status quo pending the disposal of legal challenges before the court.
The Negri Sembilan adat and constitutional crisis has drawn attention after the Undang Yang Empat reportedly exercised powers to remove Tuanku Muhriz as the state ruler.
The crisis began on May 13 last year when Datuk Mubarak Dohak was removed as Undang of Sungai Ujong following allegations of multiple breaches of 'adat' and customary laws.
The Negri Sembilan assembly was dissolved yesterday.
Negri Sembilan could face a
constitutional crisis
by June 10 if the state assembly does not convene before that date as the state's Constitution requires the state assembly to meet within six months of its previous sitting, which was held on Dec 11, 2025.
In the 2023 state election, the Pakatan Harapan (PH) and Barisan Nasional (BN) alliance formed the Negri Sembilan government after securing 31 of the 36 seats in the assembly.
PH won 17 seats, while BN secured 14. Perikatan Nasional (PN) won the remaining five seats.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Strict SOPs block 50 volunteers from joining search for Jaslinda
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Standard operating procedure (SOP) restrictions have prevented roughly 50 volunteers from joining the
search and rescue
(SAR) operation for missing hiker Jaslinda Saludin.
Kelantan Forestry Mountain Guide (MGP) Roshamrizal Zahari urged the Perak Forestry Department and the state Fire and Rescue Department to ease these rules, saying that many eager volunteers include former commandos and active-duty military personnel.
"It is disheartening that nearly 50 volunteers who contacted me could not assist due to SOPs," Roshamrizal told Berita Harian.
He added that current regulations restrict the search zone to official forestry mountain guides, and his recent attempts to advocate for public hikers were unsuccessful.
A notice on the Perak MGP Facebook page confirmed that planned public participation was canceled after failing to secure safety clearance.
To address authorities' concerns, Roshamrizal proposed systematically coordinating volunteers from the operations base, beginning at the foot of the mountain.
"While authorities want to limit numbers, we can safely organise group sizes, logistics, and safety protocols right from the start," he said.
He added that while the Perak Forestry Department appreciates the public's goodwill, it is prioritising safety by limiting search access to those with specialised knowledge of the local terrain.
Perak Fire and Rescue Department assistant director Sabarozi Nor Ahmad said that volunteers must apply and undergo screening for safety and coordination.
The Tapah Fire station will log the movements of approved search teams, who must also secure entry permits for the Permanent Forest Reserve from the Forestry Department.
Jaslinda, 49, went missing on Mount Batu Putih in Tapah, Perak, on May 24.
She was part of a 14-member group, accompanied by two MGPs, that began the Trans-Spencer Chapman expedition at 2.00am on May 23 along the Pos Gedung-Mount Bah Gading-Mount Batu Putih-Kuala Woh route.
Though she and fellow hiker Mohd Hanafi Neikmad, 41, initially stopped due to health issues, Jaslinda, a private-sector clerk, reportedly pushed on toward the summit alone and was last seen on May 24.
On June 3, authorities said that
95 per cent of the area
had been searched and the mission would be scaled down.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Work today, gone tomorrow: Bukit Aman warns of maid scam
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Police have warned of a scam syndicate that places maids with employers before they abscond after just a few days of work.
Bukit Aman Commercial Crime Investigation Department director Datuk Rusdi Mohd Isa said the syndicate lures victims by offering fast maid placement services, attractive deposit rates and seemingly legitimate documents.
"The maids show up and work for a few days to gain the victim's trust.
"However, after a few days, the maid disappears and the agent can no longer be contacted," he said in a statement.
Rusdi said victims not only lose their deposits and other payments, but may also suffer substantial financial losses as a result of the scam.
He said the syndicate often requests additional payments under various pretexts after securing the victim's trust.
"Do not let scammers take advantage of your trust. Always verify, stay alert and report immediately if you become a victim," he said.
He advised the public to verify an agency's licence before engaging its maid placement services.
"The public is also advised not to make payments to personal bank accounts and to verify phone numbers and bank accounts through the Semak Mule portal.
"Keep all proof of transactions and conversations. If you fall victim to a scam, immediately contact the National Scam Response Centre at 997," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] PM: RM50mil allocated for TVET 2.0 but companies must do their part
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The government has allocated RM50 million from the training programme fund under the Skills Development Fund Corporation (PTPPK) for the TVET 2.0 initiative launched here today, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said.
Anwar highlighted that the allocation came with a firm condition attached: employers and industry players must cooperate with the government to ensure the programme was properly coordinated and delivered results.
"Let me restate: the government is approving up to RM50 million from the high-impact programme fund under PTPK to be used for TVET 2.0.
"There is, however, a condition — employers and industry must cooperate with the government, particularly within the National TVET Council, to make it a success, so that there is proper coordination," he said at the launch of TVET 2.0 at National TVET Day 2026.
Anwar said consultations with leading firms, including Nvidia, Infineon and Amazon Web Services (AWS), had revealed that the level of training provided to local graduates did not yet meet industry requirements, particularly in information technology and cryptology-related fields.
He cited the close relationship between Infineon and Universiti Sains Malaysia (USM) as an example, highlighting that even in cases where industry-university ties were strong, engineering graduates still required exposure to additional skills disciplines, gaps that companies themselves were well-placed to fill.
Anwar called on companies to open up training opportunities for young workers, suggesting that firms could provide exposure and structured training on the condition that graduates commit to working for them upon completion.
"There are companies that have implemented such programmes, but I do not think it is widespread enough. That is why I am asking for this matter to be given priority," he said.
Anwar said the government had borne the full cost of educating and training young Malaysians from primary school through to university, only for certain private companies to absorb the graduates without contributing anything in return.
"Once trained, these young people are taken on by companies. The companies contribute no capital, no investment whatsoever.
"They simply benefit from what has already been built," he said.
Also in attendance was Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi.
Zahid, who is also National TVET Council chairman, said in his earlier speech that the launch of TVET 2.0 marked a significant step forward, building on the achievements of the previous phase while venturing into new fields including robotics, artificial intelligence, cryptology, renewable energy, maintenance, repair and overhaul (MRO) and maritime engineering.
He said TVET 2.0 would be supported by 1,345 TVET institutions nationwide spanning 12 ministries, all state governments and the private sector.
Zahid said industry partners had signed agreements with the National TVET Council and most TVET institutions nationwide guaranteeing that 100 per cent of TVET graduates would be absorbed into the workforce.
He added that the minimum salary benchmark of RM1,500 was no longer the standard, with some companies offering a minimum of RM2,500 to TVET graduates.
He also said instructors from selected TVET institutions had been sent overseas for training at no cost to the government, with expenses borne by foreign industry partners, an arrangement he described as a bilateral cooperation effort between the government and international industry.
On May 16, Zahid said the government will
restructure
the Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) curriculum, known as TVET 2.0, to strengthen the high-technology sector.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Shortage of 49 doctors behind long waiting times at Segamat Hospital
Article:
SEGAMAT: The Health Ministry has been urged to immediately deploy more personnel to Segamat Hospital to address a shortage of 49 doctors that has resulted in long waiting times for patients.
Johor Health and Environment Committee chairman Ling Tian Soon said the hospital needs 169 medical personnel to manage its various departments, several of which operate 24 hours a day.
However, only 120 personnel are currently available, putting immense pressure on the existing healthcare system.
Ling said he visited the hospital following complaints over long waiting times.
"Despite these challenges, healthcare workers continue to provide their best service," he said in a statement.
"Following discussions with hospital management and frontliners, we found that while there are 120 doctors, including specialists and medical officers, the shortage has caused longer waiting times and forced some appointments to be rescheduled," he said.
Ling added that he has contacted the ministry to request additional personnel and has held discussions with the Johor Health Department director to find short-term solutions to ease the hospital's burden.
In April, it was reported that the hospital's
emergency and trauma department
was facing a critical manpower crunch, with just 13 medical officers covering 24-hour emergency services for the district.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: BN to contest all 36 Negri Sembilan seats
Article:
JELEBU: Barisan Nasional (BN) will contest all 36 state seats in the Negri Sembilan state election, says Umno state chairman Datuk Seri Jalaluddin Alias.
He added that a list of candidates for the state election will be submitted to party president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi for review.
He said the candidates comprise both new and experienced faces, including young people and women from diverse educational backgrounds.
"BN
seat allocations
will also involve component parties MCA and MIC," he added.
x
"However, the final allocation will be decided in a meeting soon," he said when met at a wedding reception at Masjid Datuk Undang Luak Jelebu today.
Also present were Undang Luak Jelebu Datuk Maarof Mat Rashad;
Klawang assemblyman Datuk Bakri Sawir; Sungai Lui assemblyman Datuk Mohd Razi Mohd Ali; and Jelebu Islamic affairs officer and registrar of marriages, divorces and reconciliations Abdul Manas Md Isa.
Jalaluddin said all matters related to the state election will be decided in the upcoming meeting, including seats to be contested by Umno such as Jeram Padang.
The Negri Sembilan state assembly was dissolved yesterday to pave the way for the 16th state election.
The announcement was made by Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun at a special press conference late Thursday night.
In the last election, Pakatan Harapan won 17 seats, Barisan Nasional (BN) 14, and Perikatan Nasional five.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Iran strikes US bases in Kuwait, Bahrain after drone attacks on Iran territory
Article:
HAMILTON, Canada: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) said it struck "enemy bases" in Kuwait and Bahrain after United States (US) drones attacked sites on Iranian territory, Anadolu Ajansi (AA) reported, citing Tasnim News on Saturday.
The IRGC's Aerospace Force targeted the Ali al-Salem air base in Kuwait and key US Navy Fifth Fleet facilities in Bahrain with ballistic missiles, according to the semi-official news agency.
The strikes came in response to American drone attacks on a telecommunications tower on Qeshm Island and a tower in Sirik, said the IRGC.
It warned that further aggression would prompt a response beyond limited strikes, including the complete closure of the Strait of Hormuz to oil and gas shipments, rattling global energy markets and raising fears of prolonged economic damage.
Regional tensions have escalated after the US and Israel launched airstrikes on Iran in late February, triggering a cycle of retaliatory attacks that widened instability across the region.
Iran, in retaliation, subsequently launched strikes against Israel and targeted countries hosting US military bases, while also disrupting shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, a critical route for global energy supplies.
A ceasefire later took effect, though diplomatic efforts to secure a broader agreement have continued.
– BERNAMA-ANADOLU
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Secret gambling den uncovered in squatter raid; 78 foreigners detained
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: An Immigration Department operation in Jalan Klang Lama early this morning uncovered an illegal online gambling den operating within a squatter settlement following checks on about 40 homes.
The gambling outlet was discovered during Op Sapu, which led to the detention of 78 undocumented migrants.
Those detained comprised 48 men and 30 women, including Indonesians, Myanmar nationals and an Indian national.
A man found inside the gambling outlet attempted to mislead authorities when questioned.
"Lucky you came, otherwise I would have committed a sin by gambling.
"Honestly, I did not gamble. I only went in to look, but I admit my intention was to come here and learn how to play even though I know it is wrong and sinful," said the 41-year-old, who wished to be known only as Suriyanto.
The outlet was equipped with up to 20 tablet computers used for betting, as well as air-conditioning, closed-circuit television (CCTV) surveillance and a passcode-controlled entry system.
It also had a surau and a grocery shop, complete with basic utilities including water, electricity and internet access, enabling online gambling activities to operate.
Some occupants attempted to escape, while others hid under parked cars or scaled a retaining wall along a nearby highway.
Despite the poor condition of the houses, many of which were patched with tape to cover leaking roofs, undocumented migrants continued to live there.
It is understood that most residents rented rooms for as little as RM200 a month, while entire units were rented for up to RM500.
The majority of occupants were Indonesians, along with a small number of Myanmar nationals and locals.
Its secluded yet strategic location, coupled with low rental rates, made the settlement attractive to foreign workers, including undocumented migrants, as well as locals working nearby. Some were said to have lived there for decades.
About 200 people were inspected during the operation, which began at 1.30am today.
The Immigration Department also deployed a drone equipped with thermal imaging technology to detect those attempting to flee.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Onn Hafiz likely to defend Machap seat in upcoming Johor polls
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Caretaker Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi is expected to defend his Machap state seat in the
upcoming Johor elections
.
Having consistently engaged with the grassroots as menteri besar, the Johor BN chairman is widely expected to retain the mandate to lead the state for another term.
Onn Hafiz won with a majority of 6,543 votes in the previous election.
Sources say that all previously elected excos are likely to be renominated either to defend their seats or to contest in other constituencies.
There is also talk on the return of senior Umno leaders to the political scene, such as Datuk Md Jais Sarday.
However, Jais' focus is to support any BN candidate, particularly those contesting seats within the Kluang area.
"Regardless of who the candidate is, the Kluang Umno and BN machinery will support our representative," said Jais, who is also the Kluang Umno division chief.
Senator Datuk Abdul Halim Suleiman is also being tipped for candidacy in this state election.
Abdul Halim, who is the Tebrau Umno division chief, previously served as the Puteri Wangsa assemblyman from 2004 to 2013.
He is currently the Johor Umno liaison committee secretary, working closely alongside Onn Hafiz to
drive party operations
, particularly for the state chapter.
Barisan Nasional (BN) will contest all 56 seats.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Johor polls: Zahid to meet MCA, MIC tomorrow to finalise BN candidates
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi will hold discussions with the coalition's component parties tomorrow to finalise their candidates for the Johor election, with MCA and MIC still working out their positions.
"For Johor, it (the candidates) is nearly finalised. Tomorrow, I will be holding discussions with the component parties, particularly MCA and MIC, for them to finalise their positions.
"As for Umno, it is almost done. InsyaAllah, we will finalise everything fully next week, and we will make the announcement one week before nomination day," he told reporters after the opening ceremony for National TVET Day 2026 here today.
Zahid, who is also Umno president, confirmed that BN would contest all 56 seats in the Johor election.
Meanwhile on Negri Sembilan, Zahid said that BN had not closed the door on further
discussions of cooperating
with Pakatan Harapan (PH) on the state election there.
On Thursday, Zahid said BN will only unveil its candidates for the Johor election
a week before
nomination day.
He said this would ensure that issues in specific constituencies could be resolved before the candidates are announced.
On June 4, Negri Sembilan Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun said PH would contest all 36 state seats, after announcing the dissolution of the state legislative assembly that went into effect the next day.
Meanwhile on May 25, Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi said Johor BN reaffirmed their intention to contest the
state election
independently for all seats.
The last Johor election was held on March 12, 2022, called early by then-menteri besar Datuk Seri Hasni Mohammad following the dissolution of the state assembly on Jan 22.
BN won a landslide victory, capturing 40 out of 56 state seats.
PH won 12 seats while PN secured three seats and Muda one.
The Johor assembly would have automatically dissolved in April next year if elections were not called earlier.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 50 years on: Double Six tragedy a reminder to defend Sabah's rights, says Donald Mojuntin
Article:
By Olivia Miwil
Pictures by Mohd Adam Arinin
KOTA KINABALU: The 50th anniversary of the Double Six tragedy should serve as a reminder for Sabahans to remain united in defending the state's rights, dignity and future, said Moyog assemblyman Datuk Donald Peter Mojuntin.
In a statement marking the anniversary today, Donald said the tragedy, which occurred on June 6, 1976, remained one of the most significant events in Sabah's history, resulting in the loss of leaders who had devoted their lives to serving the state and its people.
"Although five decades have passed, their service, sacrifices and fighting spirit remain alive in our memory," he said.
Donald, 61, is the son of late Datuk Peter Mojuntin who was the local government and housing minister.
Peter was among the 11 victims in the plane crash that occurred off the Sembulan waters.
Other victims were the then chief minister Tun Fuad Stephens, Datuk Salleh Sulong (Sabah finance minister), Chong Thien Vun (Sabah works and communication minister), Datuk Wahid Peter Andu (secretary to Sabah finance minister), Dr Syed Hussin Wafa (state economic planning director), and Datuk Ishak Atan (private secretary to then Federal Minister Tengku Razaleigh Hamzah.
Mojuntin said the tragedy was not merely an event to be remembered but a reminder that Sabah's future must continue to be guided by courage, unity and commitment to safeguarding the state's rights and interests.
He said the same spirit continued to underpin the Sabah First movement, which he said was not about separating Sabah from Malaysia but about ensuring that the state's rights under the Malaysia Agreement 1963 (MA63) and the Federal Constitution were fully respected, implemented and protected.
"The struggle to claim Sabah's 40 per cent entitlement, powers guaranteed to the state, fair development, and respect for Sabah's position within the Federation of Malaysia, is part of the responsibility of today's generation to continue the legacy of those who came before us," he said.
Donald also called on Sabahans to remain united in facing current challenges, regardless of race, religion or political beliefs.
"Only through unity can we strengthen Sabah's voice and ensure a better future for our children," he said.
He told Sabahans to renew their resolve to defend the state's rights, dignity and future in conjunction with the 50th anniversary of the Double Six tragedy.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] PM orders all govt training bodies to open slots for TVET students
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: All government training and educational bodies will be required to provide training opportunities to TVET students nationwide, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said today.
"I am asking the Chief Secretary (Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar) to ensure that all government training and educational institutions are required to provide initial training opportunities to 50 to 100 participants at each TVET institution," he said in an officiating speech at the Hari TVET Negara, Dataran Putrajaya today.
These trainings are aimed to give participants the opportunities to build their expertise in emerging fields such as Artificial Intelligence (AI), cryptology, and renewable energy.
He said the participants will be placed into various training centres including crypto centres or other training bodies affiliated to the National Science Council.
He earlier announced that the government has allocated RM50 million from the training programme fund under the Skills Development Fund Corporation (PTPPK) for the TVET 2.0 initiative launched today.
However, he said the allocation came with a firm condition attached: employers and industry players must cooperate with the government to ensure the programme was properly coordinated and delivered results.
Anwar called on companies to open up training opportunities for young workers, suggesting that firms could provide exposure and structured training on the condition that graduates commit to working for them upon completion.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Motorcyclist killed in crash after sudden exit from Cheras junction
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A 32-year-old motorcyclist was killed in a collision with a car at a T-junction along Jalan Permaisuri in Bandar Mahkota Cheras last night.
Kajang police chief Assistant Commissioner Naazron Abdul Yusof said initial investigations indicated that the car, driven by a 35-year-old trader, was travelling along the road before approaching the junction.
"At that moment, a motorcycle ridden by a 32-year-old man suddenly emerged from the junction. The car driver could not avoid the motorcycle and collided with it," he said in a statement.
Naazron said the motorcyclist, who worked as a bus driver, was pronounced dead at the scene.
The case is being investigated under Section 41(1) of the Road Transport Act for causing death by reckless or dangerous driving.
The offence carries a prison term of between five and 10 years and a fine of RM20,000 to RM50,000 upon conviction.
Those with information on the incident are urged to contact the nearest police station or the investigating officer from the Kajang district police headquarters, Inspector Mohd Raziman Rasid, at 019-4565502 to assist in the investigation.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Police hunt men seen stealing cables in broad daylight along MRR2 [WATCH]
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Police are tracking down four men seen stealing utility cables along the Middle Ring Road 2 (MRR2) in a video that has gone viral.
Ampang Jaya police chief Assistant Commissioner Khairul Anuar Khalid said a witness saw the men loading cables into a grey Honda Odyssey on Thursday and lodged a police report.
He said checks revealed that the Honda Odyssey's registration number belonged to another vehicle.
